summary: Split from MPI-194 live testing: long-video LTX stage-gap SCALES with frames (short ~9s, 5s=45s, 10s=111s) despite the 42GB transformer staged on disk. 10s gen VRAM 17/32GB + RAM 66/92GB = aimdo VRAM<->RAM offload churn, NOT the MPI-191 volume tax. User probing 30s for OOM. Next: local repro + stock-template repro + pod-log classify. Distinct root cause; MPI-191/194 volume finding stays proven.

★ 30s data point = SUPER-LINEAR, rules out weight-read-bound. Full curve: short ~9s, 5s 45s, 10s 111s, 30s 620s (10min20s). Linear-from-10s would predict ~333s for 30s; actual 620s = ~1.9x worse. Math: full 51GB weight set from volume = only 68s; 42GB transformer from disk = 42s. 620s is ~9x any single/few weight re-read => NOT I/O-bound on weights. Points to aimdo THRASHING (repeated evict/re-fault cycles) as it juggles a working set >> 32GB VRAM on the 30s latent. Stage-1 itself = 4m30s (sampling, scales w/ frames, expected). Stage-1 VRAM stable 22.7/32, RAM 58.8/92 during sampling. CONCLUSION forming: long-video gap is aimdo dynamic-VRAM management on a 32GB card, NOT a slow-tier read -> hot-store/disk-staging CANNOT fix it. Real levers: bigger-VRAM card (48/80GB), --reserve-vram, tiled/chunked decode. Confirms MPI-197 offload-churn hypothesis over volume-tax. PENDING: did 30s survive to stage-2 or OOM? RAM peak during the 620s gap?

30s COMPLETED on 32GB (no OOM; VRAM->1.8 RAM->63.9 idle after). Full 20m52s breakdown: stage-1 4m30s + GAP 10m20s(620s) + stage-2 5m52s. THE GAP IS ~HALF THE TOTAL RUNTIME. Super-linear gap curve confirmed: 9s/45s/111s/620s for short/5s/10s/30s. Gap is aimdo evict/re-fault thrash on a working set >>32GB VRAM, NOT slow-tier I/O (620s >> 68s full-weight-set-from-volume) -> hot-store/disk-staging cannot help. SECOND finding (product, not perf): 30s output QUALITY is mediocre — wavy morphing + artifacts. Distilled/low-step LTX pipeline loses coherence at 30s; LTX 30s marketing assumes full model + 30-40 steps, not our fast tiers. => PRODUCT DECISION likely needed: cap video length or warn past N seconds, rather than chase perf on a length that also looks bad. MPI-197 reframed: (a) confirm aimdo-thrash via local + stock-template repro; (b) decide product length policy (cap/warn) given BOTH the 620s gap AND quality degradation. Levers if pursuing perf: bigger-VRAM card, --reserve-vram, tiled decode.

SAMPLER A/B (local, 3s video). CURRENT stage-2 sampler: 32s to START stage-2 (dead-gap) + 22s sampling = 54s total. LTX sampler: 15s to first step (front-loads step 1, no big dead-gap) + 25s remaining steps = 40s total. LTX ~14s faster locally BUT the shape differs crucially: current sampler pays a 32s UPFRONT GAP (evict->re-fault->reload before step 1) then fast sampling; LTX rolls straight into step 1 with no evict-everything moment. HYPOTHESIS: the current sampler dead-gap IS what thrashes aimdo super-linearly on the pod (32s local 3s -> 620s pod 30s). If LTX sampler has no big boundary gap, it may AVOID the pod blowup entirely -> could be the real MPI-197 lever + cheaper than a card-tier chase. Even locally there is a 32s gap on a 3s video, so the boundary cost is partly WORKFLOW-inherent, not purely pod/aimdo. OPEN + decisive: (1) QUALITY of LTX sampler vs current (morphing/artifacts better/same/worse?) — faster is moot if quality drops; (2) does LTX sampler kill the GAP on the POD at 10s/30s? Next: swap sampler in stage-2 workflow, A/B quality local, then pod re-test the gap curve.

SAMPLER SWAP = DEAD END (user-confirmed). 5s local A/B: NORMAL sampler 17s load + 30s sampling = 47s; LTX sampler 19s load + (16s step1 + 14s steps2-3 = 30s) = 49s. Same load, same sampling, same total, SAME QUALITY. The LTX samplers front-loads-step-1 was just a different progress BREAKDOWN, not less work — no gap advantage. Sampler choice does NOT touch the stage-boundary cost. => the pod 620s long-video gap is confirmed aimdo VRAM<->RAM thrash on 32GB, unfixable by workflow/sampler. Remaining MPI-197 levers are the expensive/structural ones ONLY: bigger-VRAM card (48/80GB so working set fits, no thrash), --reserve-vram tuning, tiled/chunked decode, OR the PRODUCT call = cap/warn video length past ~5-10s (also justified by 30s quality degradation). Local-repro done tonight (pod deleted, no more pod tests today). Next session: stock-template pod repro to confirm aimdo-inherent, then product length-policy decision.

note: Repro on official RunPod ComfyUI template started (stock stack, 5090, models -> container disk)
Stock repro: warm gaps 12s/22s (5s/10s video) vs ours 45s/111s — our-stack fault, RAM-residency lever
Round 2: v0.27.0 + taesd + 7 node packs ALL acquitted on stock (19s). Next: our image bare-launch pod (CUBRIC_COMFY_ENABLED=0) to split image vs wrapper
Round 3: bare launch on OUR image = 19s -> image+host acquitted. A/B template vs generated workflow file next

Round 4: exact-payload replays acquit wrapper, resolution, volume (warm). All pod-side axes clean. Suspect = app-driven flow (preview/event pipeline). Next: app gen + debug door, UI gap vs /history duration

summary: ★ ROUND 5 EPHEMERAL APP POD (user instinct: all app tests were volume+pinned-DC; comparisons were ephemeral). App-created EPHEMERAL pod (d6z4siy8h4z789, 5090, 125GB RAM, EU-CZ-1, disk-rooted, .expose-comfy door, v0.12.0-cu130): REAL app-driven 10s 704x1216 t2v+loras. COLD gap 32s (total 2m10s = /history 126.7s). WARM gap 31s (total 1m48s = /history 107.1s). UI-perceived == pod /history EXACTLY both runs => EVENT/PREVIEW PIPELINE ACQUITTED (last non-pod suspect dead). Same gen on VOLUME app pod = 111s warm gap => VOLUME CONVICTED as the 10s-gap multiplier. aimdo offload signature identical on disk pod (VRAM ~30->20, RAM ->67GB) but re-faults hit local NVMe = cheap. CORRECTION to 08:40 event: 'hot-store/disk-staging cannot fix it' is DISPROVEN at 10s — disk-resident deps cut the gap 111->31s in the real app flow. Reconciled model: thrash = mechanism (super-linear cycles vs frames), storage tier = per-cycle cost multiplier. OPEN: why 111s on the volume pod when MPI-194 had already disk-staged the 42GB transformer — either the loader still resolves the VOLUME copy, or the sub-15GB rest (gemma 8GB/VAEs/loras/text-proj) is re-read per thrash cycle. Fix path = MPI-191 planned full-dep-set disk hot-store (volume=library, disk=hot cache) + verify staged-copy path actually wins loader resolution. Side-note: 'loading new nodes + restarting engine' toast fired mid-gen on connect (known boot-race flag), gen unaffected.

ROUND 5b RES-SCALING ON DISK (same ephemeral pod, warm, 10s t2v+loras): 1920x1088 gap 104s / total 262.1s (/history == UI 4:23 again) vs 1216x704 gap 31s / total 107s. Pixels x2.44 -> TOTAL x2.45 (linear, sampling) but GAP x3.35 (super-linear) => even with all deps on local NVMe, aimdo thrash cycles grow with working-set size on 32GB; disk kills the per-cycle price, not the cycle count. Community reference ~82s for 20s video is on FP8 (~20GB transformer fits 32GB VRAM = little/no thrash); we run bf16 40GB. Lever list for the product call: fp8_scaled transformer variant (working set fits -> thrash gone; NOT the removed GGUF path), bigger-VRAM card tier, or res/length-aware expectations in UI. Fix for volume pods (full dep-set hot-store + staged-path resolution check) still stands independently.

★★ ROUND 6 — 111s GAP DOES NOT REPRODUCE. App-created VOLUME pod (h7lo80x4819i00, EU-RO-1, 5090, 93GB RAM Ryzen-7950X host, 50GB disk, wrapper 0.2.27, door open): real app-driven 10s 1216x704 t2v+loras. COLD gap 32s (total 3:23 = /history 201.8s incl 1m50s cold load). WARM gap 34s (total 3:00 = /history 179.1s). Same res on the morning volume pods = 111s WARM gap. Current stack = 31-34s gap on BOTH ephemeral (disk) and volume pods => the long-video 10s gap is FIXED on today's runtime. Between the 111s measurements (~08:00) and tonight, FOUR runtime fixes shipped 03:30-04:46 (R2-fetched at boot, so only NEW pods get them... timeline of morning pod creation vs publishes UNRESOLVED): wrapper 0.2.24 event-loop fixes (nvidia-smi off-loop — old blocking stats poll contended with UVM fault storm ONLY when app connected, invisible to every bare/replay test), MPI-193 volume node-pack dedupe (tonight's boot log CLEAN — no dup packs; stale volume LTX packs were loaded on morning-era pods), MPI-194 hot-store + dryRun fix (staging confirmed live tonight: 'already copied' + disk gauge). Attribution between the three not isolated (would need a deliberately-old-runtime pod; low value). VOLUME-vs-DISK residual: warm TOTAL 179s vs ephemeral 107s (gap same 34 vs 31) — sampling/decode slower on this host or small-model volume reads; secondary, not the gap. App flow: 5/5 runs UI==pod /history exactly => app/event/preview pipeline PERMANENTLY ACQUITTED. OPEN: (1) re-test 20s/30s length scaling on fixed stack (morning 620s@30s was old stack), (2) product length policy (30s quality still mediocre), (3) cleanup: delete .expose-comfy marker + terminate test pods when session done.

ROUND 6b LENGTH SCALING ON FIXED STACK (same volume pod, warm, 1216x704 t2v+loras+audio): 20s video gap 77s / total 209.2s (/history; UI matched again). vs 34s gap @10s => x2.26 gap for x2 length — mildly super-linear (+13% over linear), NOT the old-stack explosion (morning old-runtime curve: 45s@5s -> 111s@10s -> 620s@30s GAP). Extrapolated 30s gap on fixed stack ~2min vs 620s measured this morning. Pile-up real but shallow; product length-cap decision can now weigh QUALITY (30s output mediocre, wavy morphing) as the primary axis rather than perf.

★★★ ROUND 7 FINAL DIAGNOSIS (pod /internal/logs/raw = per-phase timestamps, all 5 runs on volume pod h7lo80x4819i00): APP TAX = ZERO. Server-side phases app vs browser IDENTICAL: stage-2 boundary region 48.9/48.5/48.4s across app/browser/app runs; step-1 40GB fault-in = monotonic page-cache warming 44->39.6->24->23.5->20.3s regardless of submitter (run 5 through the APP was the day's fastest: 113.7s total). User-perceived UI gaps (34/17/33s) measure event-visibility windows, NOT execution — UI gap is NOT a valid metric; pod log is. THE ONE REMAINING COST, named by the log: at stage boundary ComfyUI logs 'Requested to load LTXAV ... 0 patches attached' — stage-2 runs LORA-LESS, ComfyUI treats unpatched model as a DIFFERENT model -> full 40GB re-stage mid-prompt (~48.5s constant @10s, 116s @20s = also carries stage-2 sampling). Same mechanism as the known LoRA law (bypass reloads model -> OOM; strength flip doesn't). FIX (user to implement, workflow structural edit): stage-2 gets the SAME lora stack attached with strength 0 (mathematically = no lora, keeps model object, no re-stage) -> boundary should collapse to single digits. Verify by re-running on a door pod and confirming the 0-patches reload line is gone.

ROUND 8 — LORA-REWIRE HYPOTHESIS DISPROVEN LIVE (user browser test, run 63b67f6d, same pod): stage-2 guiders rewired to the lora'd model output (['191',0] — payload-verified, '0 patches' line GONE) → boundary region WORSENED 48.4s -> 63.2s, total 113.7 -> 130.4s. The 40050MB 'Staged' line fires at stage-2 regardless of patch set (it also fires mid-stage-1 cheaply) — the reload was never patch-mismatch-triggered. Stage-2 with 3544 patches attached pays lora application per faulted weight at upscaled res = +15s. REVERT: keep stage-2 lora-less (edit was browser-tab-only; repo untouched). Boundary floor decomposition: legit inter-stage work (latent upsample + audio path + VAE) + one warm ~20s 40GB re-fault after between-stage eviction — structural on 32GB with a 40GB bf16 model. True kills remain: fp8_scaled transformer (fits 32GB, no evict) or bigger-VRAM tier. MPI-197 perf investigation COMPLETE: old-stack 111s/620s solved by shipped runtime fixes; current floor 48s@10s / 116s@20s boundary is architecture, not bug.

USER-VERIFIED CLOSE: user's own repeated app-vs-browser total-time A/Bs confirm — always very close, app sometimes FASTER. App pipeline verdict final. TIER DECISION (user): balanced tier = Kijai distilled-1.1 mxfp8_block32 (Blackwell, 40%-faster PR benchmark) + fp8_scaled (Ada/universal); NVFP4 rejected (user-read reviews + research agree: artifacts, i2v broken); LOW TIER DEFERRED (would need Q4/GGUF — not re-opening now). bf16 stays quality tier. Full variant research in research/quant-tiers.md. Remaining: card the tier feature separately; MPI-197 itself is investigation-complete + user-verified.
User-verified: gap fixed on current runtime (34s@10s / 77s@20s), app pipeline acquitted 6/6 + user A/Bs (app==browser, sometimes faster).
Archived completed card during board cleanup.
